conversations.content: What is the purpose of the leave policy at TechMojo?

At TechMojo, we understand that taking time off for personal reasons is essential for your overall well-being and helps you recharge. Our leave policy is designed to support you in achieving this balance, allowing you to tend to important personal matters without impacting your work responsibilities. By taking planned leaves, you can ensure a smoother transition of tasks and minimize disruptions to our team's productivity.

**TechMojo policy:** The purpose of the leave policy at TechMojo is to help employees maintain a balance between work and personal life while encouraging planned leaves to avoid disruptions in work.
Who is eligible for the leave policy at TechMojo?

To be eligible for our leave policy, you must be a confirmed employee of TechMojo, meaning you've completed your probationary period and are fully on board with our team. This ensures that all employees have a fair and consistent experience with our leave policy, while also giving us time to assess your performance and fit within the company. As a confirmed employee, you'll be able to take advantage of the benefits and guidelines outlined in our leave policy.

**TechMojo policy:** The leave policy is applicable to all confirmed employees at TechMojo. It does not apply to employees under probation.
How many annual leave days can an employee carry forward to the next year?

To help you make the most of your time off, we allow you to carry forward up to 5 unused annual leave days into the next year. This means you can save some of your leave days for a future vacation or break, as long as you use them by the end of the following year. Keep in mind that any unused days beyond that will expire at the end of the year.

**TechMojo policy:** An employee can carry forward up to 5 days of unutilized leave into the next year. These leaves will lapse at the end of the succeeding year if not used.
What happens to unused leave days exceeding 5 days?

At the end of each year, any unused leave days that exceed the maximum allowed 5 days will be forfeited and will not carry over into the new year. This means that if you have more than 5 unused leave days at the end of the year, you will lose those extra days and will not be able to use them in the future. This policy is in place to ensure fairness and consistency in leave usage among all employees.

**TechMojo policy:** Any unutilized leave beyond 5 days will automatically lapse by the end of the year.
What is the rule for applying planned leaves?

To ensure that your team's workload is properly covered and that we can make necessary arrangements, we require at least 7 days' notice for any planned leaves, such as vacation or a scheduled appointment. This allows us to make the necessary arrangements and minimize any disruptions to our operations. Please submit your leave request to your supervisor at least 7 days in advance for approval.

**TechMojo policy:** Planned leaves must be applied at least 7 calendar days in advance and require prior written approval from the immediate supervisor.
How many unplanned leaves are allowed for medical emergencies?

We understand that unexpected medical emergencies can be stressful and impact your work. That's why we have a policy in place to support you during these challenging times, allowing up to 3 unplanned leaves for immediate family members without impacting your pay. However, if you need more time off, please let us know as soon as possible so we can work together to find a solution.

**TechMojo policy:** A maximum of 3 unplanned leaves are allowed for medical emergencies within the immediate family. Beyond that, it will result in loss of pay and proof must be submitted to the supervisor and HR.
What should an employee do in case of an unplanned leave?

In the event of an unplanned leave, we want to ensure that our clients are informed and our team can adjust accordingly. This means that if you need to take an unexpected day off, you'll need to let your local manager, our HR team, and the client team manager (if you work with clients) know as soon as possible by sending them an email. This helps us manage our workload and communicate with our clients on your behalf.

**TechMojo policy:** The employee must inform their local manager at TechMojo, HR, and the client team manager (if applicable) by email.
Where should employees apply for their leaves?

To ensure that your leave requests are processed accurately and efficiently, we ask that you submit your leave applications through the dedicated leave management system at https://people.techmojo.com. This system allows you to easily submit and track your leave requests, and helps our HR team to manage leave balances and approvals. By using this system, you'll be able to keep your leave history up-to-date and avoid any misunderstandings about your leave status.

**TechMojo policy:** Employees must apply for their leaves through the leave management system at https://people.techmojo.com.
When is LOP (Loss of Pay) calculated?

At the end of our financial year, we calculate the Loss of Pay (LOP) based on any unpaid leaves taken during the year. This ensures that the company's payroll is accurate and reflects the actual time worked by employees. The LOP is then deducted from your salary in March, which is the first time we pay out the full year's salary.

**TechMojo policy:** LOP is calculated at the end of the financial year and deducted from the salary payable in March.
What is the total number of leaves an employee gets in a year at TechMojo?

At TechMojo, we want to ensure that you have enough time to relax and recharge, which is why we offer a generous annual leave policy. This allows you to take up to 20 days off each year to pursue your interests, attend to personal matters, or simply take a well-deserved break. This leave can be taken in advance or saved for later, giving you flexibility to plan your time off around your needs.

**TechMojo policy:** An employee is eligible for a total of 20 days of annual leave in a year at TechMojo.
When can an employee take unplanned leave?

In the event of a medical emergency affecting a family member, we understand that unexpected time off may be necessary. This policy allows you to take unplanned leave to care for your loved ones in their time of need, up to a maximum of three instances. Please note that proof of the emergency will be required, and we're here to support you through this challenging situation.

**TechMojo policy:** An employee can take unplanned leave only in case of medical emergencies within the immediate family. A maximum of 3 such leaves are allowed and require proof of emergency.
Can employees work from home instead of applying for leave?

While we understand that some days may not be ideal for commuting, our leave policy is designed to ensure that all employees have a consistent and predictable schedule, which is crucial for our team's collaboration and overall performance. To maintain this consistency, we require all employees to apply for leave even when working from home. This allows us to plan and adjust our team's workload accordingly.

**TechMojo policy:** No, employees who want to work from home must still apply for a full day of leave.
Can I take leave without prior approval?

We want to ensure that our teams are always prepared to meet our commitments to customers and colleagues, so we need to plan ahead for leaves and absences. This means giving your supervisor enough time to adjust schedules and make necessary arrangements, which is why prior approval is required. By giving 7 days' notice, you're helping us maintain a smooth and efficient work flow.

**TechMojo policy:** No, all planned leaves must be applied at least 7 calendar days in advance and require prior written approval from your immediate supervisor.
How should I apply for leave at TechMojo?

To ensure that your leave is accurately recorded and approved in a timely manner, we ask that you submit your leave requests through the dedicated leave management system at https://people.techmojo.com. This centralized system allows managers to review and approve leave requests efficiently, helping us maintain a smooth workflow across teams. By using this system, you'll also receive reminders and notifications about your leave status, making it easier to plan your work and time off.

**TechMojo policy:** You should apply for leave through the official leave management system at https://people.techmojo.com.
What should I do if I need to take an unplanned leave?

If you need to take an unplanned leave, it's essential to let your team know as soon as possible so we can adjust our plans and ensure everything runs smoothly. To do this, please send an email to your local manager, the HR team, and the client team manager (if your work involves a client) to let them know what's happening and how long you expect to be out. This helps us minimize disruptions and make necessary arrangements, so your absence doesn't impact our projects or customers.

**TechMojo policy:** You must immediately inform your local manager at TechMojo, HR, and the client team manager (if applicable) by email.
What happens if I take more leave than I have accrued?

If you find yourself needing to take more leave than you've accumulated, please note that this situation can arise due to unforeseen circumstances or miscalculations. In such cases, the company will need to adjust your leave balance accordingly, which may result in a deduction from your March salary. This ensures fairness and consistency in our leave policies, and we're here to support you through any challenges that may come up.

**TechMojo policy:** If you take more leave than you have accrued, the extra days will be treated as Loss of Pay (LOP) and deducted from your March salary.
Can I apply for a half day of leave?

We understand that sometimes you might need to take a shorter leave, but for administrative and operational reasons, we can't accommodate half-day leaves. This means you'll need to take the whole day off when you apply for leave, and we encourage you to plan ahead and use our ADP portal to submit your requests in advance. This helps us ensure that we're adequately staffed and prepared for our customers and internal operations.

**TechMojo policy:** No, TechMojo does not have a half-day leave policy. All leaves must be taken as full-day leaves and applied through the ADP portal.
Will unused leaves accumulate year after year?

To help you plan and make the most of your time off, we've implemented a rule that limits the number of unused leave days you can carry over to the next year. This means you can bring up to 5 unused days with you into the new year, but any extra days will expire at the end of the current year, so it's a good idea to use them or make a plan to use them before the year ends. This approach helps us manage our leave policies and ensure everyone has a fair and consistent experience.

**TechMojo policy:** No. You may carry forward a maximum of 5 unutilized leave days to the next year, and they will lapse if not used by the end of the succeeding year. Any leave beyond 5 days will lapse at the end of the current year.
How is leave calculated during the year?

Here's the explanation:

Leave is calculated based on the number of working days you've completed in the year, so you'll have a certain amount of leave available to you as you work throughout the year. This means you can take leave, but only up to the amount you've actually earned, so it's essential to keep track of your accrued leave to avoid any issues. This approach helps ensure fairness and consistency in leave allocation across the year.

**TechMojo policy:** Leave is accrued on a pro-rata basis based on the number of working days served in the financial year. You can only avail leave that you have already accrued.
Do I need to provide proof for unplanned medical leave?

When taking unplanned leave for a medical emergency, we understand that you may not have time to gather documentation beforehand. In these situations, it's essential to provide proof of the medical emergency as soon as possible to ensure we can support you during your leave and make arrangements for your work responsibilities. This helps us to verify the validity of your leave and ensure continuity of our operations.

**TechMojo policy:** Yes, you must provide proof of the medical emergency to your immediate supervisor and HR if you take unplanned leave for medical reasons.
Is there any leave encashment policy at TechMojo?

At TechMojo, we want to ensure that you have a good balance between work and personal life. That's why we have a limit on the number of unused vacation days you can carry forward from one year to the next. Unfortunately, if you don't use up all your eligible leave within that timeframe, any excess will expire, so be sure to plan ahead and take your days off when you can.

**TechMojo policy:** No, TechMojo does not mention any leave encashment policy. Unused leaves beyond the carry forward limit will lapse.
What are the eligibility criteria for maternity leave?

To ensure we're supporting you during this special time, our maternity leave policy requires that you've been a full-time employee with us for at least 80 days prior to your expected delivery date. This allows us to provide a stable and supportive work environment for you, while also ensuring we're following the necessary procedures to provide you with the benefits you're eligible for. By meeting this eligibility criteria, you can focus on taking care of yourself and your growing family, knowing that we've got your back.

**TechMojo policy:** A full-time female employee must have worked at Tech Mojo Solutions for at least 80 days immediately before the expected delivery date.
How much paid maternity leave can I take?

Our paid maternity leave policy is designed to support you during this important life moment, allowing you to take the time you need to care for your newborn and establish a strong bond. The amount of leave you're eligible for increases with each child, reflecting our company's commitment to helping you balance work and family responsibilities. This policy aims to provide a balance between supporting your family needs and ensuring business continuity.

**TechMojo policy:** You can take up to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave for up to two surviving children. For the third child, you are eligible for 12 weeks of paid leave.
Can I begin maternity leave before my delivery date?

Maternity leave is designed to provide a supportive environment for you to prepare for the arrival of your baby, both physically and emotionally. To ensure that you have adequate time to do so, we allow you to start your maternity leave before your expected delivery date, with the amount of time varying based on the number of children you have. This flexibility helps you to balance your work and family responsibilities during this important life transition.

**TechMojo policy:** Yes. For up to two children, up to 8 weeks can precede the expected delivery date. For the third child, up to 6 weeks can precede the delivery date.
Do I need to notify anyone before going on maternity leave?

To ensure a smooth transition of your responsibilities and allow us to make necessary arrangements, it's essential to let your manager know about your upcoming maternity leave well in advance. This will give us time to find a suitable replacement or adjust your workload accordingly, which benefits both you and the team. By giving us sufficient notice, you'll also be able to complete any outstanding tasks and prepare for your leave with minimal disruption.

**TechMojo policy:** Yes, you need to inform your manager well in advance and send an email regarding your maternity leave.
What happens to my unused leave if I take full maternity leave?

When you take a full 6 months of maternity leave, any accrued leave time you haven't used up will automatically expire, as per our company's policies. This is to ensure fairness and consistency for all employees, and to avoid any confusion about leave balances when you return to work. This way, you can focus on your well-being and recovery during your leave, without worrying about tracking your leave time.

**TechMojo policy:** If you take 6 months of maternity leave, any unused accrued leave will lapse.
Can I keep my unused leave during maternity leave?

When planning for your maternity leave, it's essential to note that taking the full 6 months off will mean that any unused accrued leave will expire at the end of your leave period. This is because our leave policies are designed to ensure that employees take the time they need to recover and care for their newborn, rather than carrying over unused leave from previous periods. This means that you'll want to use up any available leave before or after your maternity leave, if possible.

**TechMojo policy:** No, unused accrued leave will lapse if you take the full 6 months of maternity leave.
Will I receive a bonus during my maternity leave?

During your maternity leave, your compensation will include your fixed salary, which is paid according to the applicable laws and regulations. This is to ensure you receive a steady income during this time. However, the variable bonus, which is tied to your performance during the financial year, will not be paid out during your leave period.

**TechMojo policy:** You will not be eligible for the discretionary performance-linked variable bonus during the financial year in which you take maternity leave. However, fixed salary will be paid as per statutory norms.
Am I eligible for appraisal while on maternity leave?

Our appraisal process is designed to evaluate an employee's performance over a specific period, and maternity leave is a time for you to focus on your well-being and your new baby. As a result, we've made the decision to pause the appraisal process during your maternity leave, so you can focus on this important life event without any added stress or responsibilities. You'll be eligible for the next appraisal cycle, which will give you a chance to demonstrate your skills and accomplishments when you're ready to return to work.

**TechMojo policy:** No, you are not eligible for the appraisal process during your maternity break. You will be eligible in the next appraisal cycle.
Can I take more leave than I’ve earned?

To ensure fairness and transparency in our leave policy, we require employees to take only the amount of leave they've accrued, rather than borrowing from future accruals. This approach helps prevent financial uncertainty and ensures that everyone is treated equally. By respecting the leave balance you've earned, we can maintain a healthy and sustainable leave system for everyone.

**TechMojo policy:** No, any leave taken beyond what you have accrued will result in loss of pay.
Can employees work from home instead of applying for leave?

We understand that sometimes life gets in the way and a flexible schedule is needed, but we have a policy in place to ensure fairness and consistency for everyone. This means that even if you're working from home, you still need to apply for a full day of leave so we can keep track of your time and make sure you're not overcommitting yourself. This helps us maintain a healthy work-life balance and ensures that everyone gets the time off they need.

**TechMojo policy:** No, employees who want to work from home must still apply for a full day of leave.
How many children does the 26-week maternity leave apply to?

This policy is designed to support employees in balancing their family responsibilities and work commitments, particularly during the critical period of caring for a newborn. The eligibility for the 26-week paid maternity leave extends to employees who have had two or more surviving children, ensuring that they can take the necessary time off to care for their growing family. This support is intended to help employees recharge and return to work with renewed energy and focus.

**TechMojo policy:** The 26-week paid maternity leave is applicable for up to two surviving children.
How much maternity leave is given for the third child?

As a valued employee, you're entitled to a generous maternity leave policy to support you during this special time. Our company recognizes the importance of bonding with your third child and provides 12 weeks of maternity leave to ensure you have sufficient time to care for them. This comprehensive leave allows you to focus on your family's needs without worrying about work responsibilities.

**TechMojo policy:** For the third child, the maternity leave entitlement is 12 weeks.
How much of the maternity leave can I take before delivery?

We want to support you during this exciting time, and that includes giving you advance notice to plan for your leave. Our policy allows you to take up to 8 weeks of maternity leave before the expected delivery date for your first two children, and up to 6 weeks for your third child, to ensure you have enough time to prepare for your baby's arrival. This way, you can make necessary arrangements and enjoy some extra time with your family before the baby comes.

**TechMojo policy:** You can take up to 8 weeks of leave before the expected delivery date for the first two children, and up to 6 weeks before the delivery for the third child.
Do I need to send an email for maternity leave?

To ensure a smooth transition of your responsibilities and allow us to make necessary arrangements, it's essential to give your manager advance notice of your maternity leave, and a formal email is the best way to do this. This allows us to plan ahead, communicate with your team, and make any necessary adjustments to our schedule. By sending a formal email, you're helping us to support you during this important time.

**TechMojo policy:** Yes, you are required to inform your manager in advance and formally request maternity leave via email.
Will my unused leave carry over after maternity leave?

When you take the full 6 months of maternity leave, it's essential to note that any remaining accrued leave will automatically expire. This means that after your maternity leave, you won't have any leftover time to use for vacation or other types of leave. This policy helps us ensure that all employees have a clear understanding of their leave balances during and after their maternity leave.

**TechMojo policy:** No, if you take the full 6 months of maternity leave, any remaining accrued leave will lapse.
Will I get paid during maternity leave?

During your maternity leave, you will continue to receive your regular fixed salary, without any reduction, as it is a statutory requirement for employers to provide paid leave to new mothers. This ensures that you have financial support during this important time. Our company complies with these regulations to support you and your family.

**TechMojo policy:** Yes, fixed salary will be paid as per statutory norms during your maternity leave.
Can I take unpaid leave if I have exhausted my maternity leave?

When you've reached the end of your designated maternity leave, it's natural to feel uncertain about your next steps. This policy is in place to ensure we provide a clear and structured approach to supporting you during this important time, and we're here to help you explore your options. Please don't hesitate to reach out to HR to discuss your specific situation and determine the best course of action for you.

**TechMojo policy:** The policy does not mention unpaid maternity leave. You should check with HR for options beyond the allowed 26 or 12 weeks.
Will I get my appraisal while on maternity leave?

Our appraisal process is designed to evaluate performance over a specific period, and we want to ensure that you have the time and support you need during your maternity leave. As a result, we've made the decision to exclude employees on maternity leave from the current cycle's appraisal process, allowing you to focus on your well-being and new baby. You'll be considered for the next cycle's appraisal, when you'll have had time to return to work and settle back into your role.

**TechMojo policy:** No, employees on maternity leave are not eligible for the appraisal process during that cycle. They will be considered in the next appraisal.
Can I combine my earned leave with maternity leave?

To ensure that all employees receive the necessary support during maternity leave, our company has a policy in place that allows for a full, paid leave experience. This means that you'll receive your full pay for the duration of your maternity leave, without any deductions for unearned leave. By keeping earned leave separate from maternity leave, we can provide a clear and predictable financial experience for new mothers.

**TechMojo policy:** No, you cannot use unearned leave during maternity leave. Any leave taken beyond your accrual will lead to loss of pay.
If I work from home during maternity leave, will it count as leave?

During your maternity leave, if you're able to work from home, it's essential to remember that this time is still counted as part of your leave under our policy. This means that even though you may be performing some work tasks from home, you're still entitled to the same leave benefits and support as you would be if you were on a traditional leave. This policy helps ensure consistency and fairness for all employees during this important time.

**TechMojo policy:** Yes, any work from home during maternity leave is still considered leave under the policy.
What is the purpose of sabbatical leave?

Our sabbatical leave policy is designed to support your overall well-being and personal growth, whether that's taking time to focus on your health, pursuing a new skill or interest, or simply stepping back to recharge. This break can be a valuable opportunity to refresh your perspective, explore new opportunities, or simply take a much-needed pause. By offering this extended leave, we're committed to supporting your holistic development and success.

**TechMojo policy:** Sabbatical leave allows employees to take an extended break for personal medical reasons, professional growth, research, or any approved personal purpose. It supports employee well-being and development.
Who is eligible for sabbatical leave?

To be eligible for sabbatical leave, you'll need to have been with TechMojo for at least a year, consistently delivering high-quality work and meeting performance expectations. This break is intended to give you time to recharge and pursue personal interests, so we want to make sure you've had a chance to settle into your role and demonstrate your capabilities. By meeting these criteria, you'll be able to take advantage of this opportunity and return to work refreshed and revitalized.

**TechMojo policy:** Full-time employees with at least one year of continuous service, good performance, and no disciplinary action in the past year are eligible.
How long can sabbatical leave last?

When planning a sabbatical, you'll have the opportunity to propose a leave duration that meets your personal needs and goals, while also ensuring it aligns with our company's operational requirements. This allows us to support your well-being and personal growth while also minimizing the impact on our team's workload and projects. By working together, we can find a mutually beneficial length for your sabbatical leave.

**TechMojo policy:** Sabbatical leave can last from one to three months, based on the employee’s proposal and company requirements.
What is the maximum duration of sabbatical leave?

Sabbatical leave is a valuable benefit that allows you to take a break from work to recharge, pursue personal interests, or attend to family needs. This leave is designed to give you a chance to step back, relax, and come back to your role refreshed and revitalized, which is why we've established a maximum duration to ensure a smooth transition of responsibilities during your absence.

**TechMojo policy:** The maximum duration of sabbatical leave is 3 months.
On what basis will my sabbatical leave duration be decided?

When it's time for your sabbatical, we'll work together to determine the right length of time for you to take off. Your proposed duration will be carefully considered, taking into account the impact on our team's workload and responsibilities. By collaborating with you, we can find a solution that meets both your personal needs and the needs of the organization.

**TechMojo policy:** The duration of your sabbatical leave will be determined based on your proposal and the organization’s operational requirements.
What do I need to include in the application for sabbatical leave?

To apply for sabbatical leave, you'll need to submit a detailed application that outlines your plans for the time you'll be taking off. This includes specifying how long you'd like your sabbatical to last and what you hope to achieve during that time, whether it's for personal, educational, or medical reasons. By providing this information, you'll ensure that your application is complete and that we can review it to determine whether your request can be approved.

**TechMojo policy:** You need to include the proposed duration, the purpose of the sabbatical, relevant medical reports if any, and send an email to your lead and HR.
How far in advance should I apply for sabbatical leave?

To ensure that we can accommodate your sabbatical leave request, it's essential to plan ahead and give us sufficient time to review and approve your application. That's why we require you to submit your request at least one month before your desired start date, allowing us to make the necessary arrangements and confirm the leave with the relevant teams. This advance notice helps us to minimize disruptions to our operations and ensure a smooth transition of responsibilities during your absence.

**TechMojo policy:** You must apply at least one month before the intended start date.
Is HR approval alone enough for sabbatical leave?

Before you can take a sabbatical leave, we want to ensure that both your lead and HR are aware of your plans and have given their approval. This means getting a thumbs up from your direct supervisor and a confirmation from HR that everything is in order, which we require to be documented via email. This extra step helps us guarantee that your team is prepared for your absence and that you have a smooth transition before you head out on your break.

**TechMojo policy:** No, you need approval from both your lead and HR via email before you can start the leave.
What happens after my sabbatical leave is approved?

Once your sabbatical leave is approved by both your lead and HR, you'll receive official confirmation via email, which marks the final step in the approval process. At this point, you can start making arrangements for your leave, knowing that all necessary approvals are in place. This confirmation also serves as a formal notification to your team and stakeholders that your leave has been approved.

**TechMojo policy:** Once you get approval from both your lead and HR through email, you become eligible to start your sabbatical leave.
Can I take sabbatical leave for personal reasons?

Sabbatical leave is designed to give you the time and space you need to focus on your personal well-being, pursue new interests, or enhance your professional skills. This break can be tailored to your specific needs, whether it's for medical recovery, research, or simply taking a well-deserved pause. By offering sabbatical leave, we're committed to supporting your overall growth and happiness.

**TechMojo policy:** Yes, sabbatical leave can be taken for personal medical reasons, research, professional development, or any personal break approved by management.
What if my job performance is not satisfactory?

To be eligible for a sabbatical leave, we need to see that you've been doing a great job in your role, consistently meeting or exceeding expectations. This means you've been delivering high-quality results, taking on new challenges, and contributing positively to the team. By requiring satisfactory job performance, we're ensuring that our sabbatical leave policy supports employees who are already making significant contributions to our organization.

**TechMojo policy:** Employees must have demonstrated satisfactory job performance to be eligible for sabbatical leave.
What if I had a disciplinary action in the past year?

If you had a disciplinary action within the past year, it's essential to remember that it's not a reflection on your current performance or potential. However, our company's sabbatical leave policy aims to support employees who have demonstrated a strong track record of responsible behavior and adherence to our company's expectations. Unfortunately, this means that you wouldn't be eligible for sabbatical leave at this time.

**TechMojo policy:** Employees with a disciplinary record in the last year are not eligible for sabbatical leave.
Who should I send my sabbatical leave email to?

When it's time for your sabbatical, we want to ensure that everyone involved is aware of your plans and can help support you throughout the process. To do this, please submit your sabbatical leave request to both your direct supervisor, who will help facilitate any necessary team adjustments, and to HR, who will guide you through the formal approval process and any related benefits or next steps. This dual notification helps us keep everyone informed and working together to make your sabbatical a success.

**TechMojo policy:** You must send your sabbatical leave email request to both your reporting lead and HR.
Is it mandatory to submit medical reports?

When taking a sabbatical for medical reasons, we require medical reports to ensure we have a clear understanding of your situation and to maintain accurate internal records. This helps us to better support you during your leave and to ensure a smooth transition of your responsibilities while you're away. Submitting these reports is an important step in the sabbatical process for medical leaves.

**TechMojo policy:** Yes, if your sabbatical is for medical reasons, you must submit medical reports to HR for internal records.
What should be in the sabbatical leave plan?

When planning your sabbatical, it's essential to think about the purpose and duration of your leave, as well as any specific requirements you may need to fulfill, such as providing medical reports or study outlines, to ensure a smooth and successful experience. This upfront planning will help you stay focused and motivated during your time off, and also help us support you better during your leave. By having a clear plan in place, you can make the most of your sabbatical and return to work refreshed and revitalized.

**TechMojo policy:** Your sabbatical plan should clearly explain the purpose, duration, and any relevant supporting documents like medical reports or study outlines.
Who should I reach out to if I'm having trouble with my laptop?

If you're experiencing any issues with your company-issued laptop, such as connectivity problems or software glitches, don't hesitate to reach out to our dedicated IT support team for assistance. They're here to help you troubleshoot and resolve any technical difficulties, ensuring you can stay productive and focused on your work. By contacting them, you can get back to what matters most – delivering great results for TechMojo.

**TechMojo policy:** Contact Mr. Naveen Peddi at 9966088494, Mr. Govardhan, or email it-helpdesk@techmojo.in for technical support.
I'm facing issues with VPN access. Who can help me?

If you're having trouble accessing our company's VPN, don't worry - we've got you covered. Our IT team is here to help resolve any technical issues, and they can be reached through multiple channels for your convenience. You can either contact Mr. Naveen Peddi directly, reach out to Mr. Govardhan, or send an email to our dedicated helpdesk for assistance.

**TechMojo policy:** Contact Mr. Naveen Peddi at 9966088494, Mr. Govardhan, or email it-helpdesk@techmojo.in for technical support.
My desktop is not working properly. Who do I inform?

If your desktop is experiencing issues, don't worry - we've got you covered. When your computer isn't working as expected, it's essential to let our IT team know so they can send help your way. To do this, simply reach out to Mr. Naveen Peddi, Mr. Govardhan, or email the IT helpdesk team.

**TechMojo policy:** Contact Mr. Naveen Peddi at 9966088494, Mr. Govardhan, or email it-helpdesk@techmojo.in for technical support.
Whom should I contact for any network issues at work?

If you're experiencing any network issues at work, such as trouble connecting to the internet or accessing company resources, don't hesitate to reach out to our IT support team for assistance. As part of this review, you can also expect to receive any additional compensation you're entitled to for the time you've spent with us so far.

**TechMojo policy:** Your appraisal meeting will be scheduled the next month after completing one year. The compensation will include arrears from the day you completed your first year.

Can I encash my leftover leaves at the end of the year?

At the end of the year, you won't be able to encash any leftover leaves. However, you do have the option to carry forward up to 5 unused leaves into the next year, which can be a helpful way to make the most of your paid time off. This means you can use these extra leaves during the following year, rather than losing them.

**TechMojo policy:** There is no leave encashment. Only up to 5 leaves can be carried forward to the next year, regardless of whether they are planned or unplanned.

This policy helps us support your flexibility and well-being, while also allowing you to plan for your time off in advance.

**TechMojo policy:** There is no leave encashment. Only up to 5 leaves can be carried forward to the next year, regardless of whether they are planned or unplanned.

**TechMojo policy:** There is no leave encashment. Only up to 5 leaves can be carried forward to the next year, regardless of whether they are planned or unplanned.

Our annual leave cycle runs from April to March, which means that your leave balance will be reset and your carryover limit will be applied at the end of March every year. This cycle allows us to manage leave effectively and ensures that everyone has a fair opportunity to take time off during the year. By having a consistent cycle, we can also plan and budget for our leave needs more efficiently.

**TechMojo policy:** The leave cycle is from April to March.

The leave year, which determines how much paid time off you're entitled to, runs from April 1st to March 31st of the following year. This means that any unused leave time from the previous year carries over into the new year, and you'll start fresh on April 1st. This cycle helps us manage leave balances and ensure everyone gets the time they need to recharge.

**TechMojo policy:** The leave cycle is from April to March.

Do we get public holidays off in production support?

As a member of our production support team, you play a critical role in ensuring our services are always available to our customers, 24/7. This means that even on public holidays, our systems and services must continue to run without interruption. In recognition of the extra effort you put in to support our operations during these times, we offer flexi holidays as a way to give you a break and some extra time off.

**TechMojo policy:** Since production support works round the clock, support activities do not stop on public holidays. Flexi holidays are provided as compensation.
What is a Flexi Holiday in production support?

When a public holiday falls on a day that's not your usual day off, you get to take an extra day off instead – this is called a Flexi Holiday. This policy helps ensure that you still get to enjoy your day off, even if the public holiday lands on a different day. By granting a Flexi Holiday, we're giving you more flexibility to plan your time off and make the most of your days off.

**TechMojo policy:** A Flexi Holiday is granted when a production support employee works on a public holiday that does not fall on their weekly off.
Who approves Flexi Holidays?

Flexi holidays are a great perk that allows you to take a day off when you need it most, but to ensure fairness and avoid last-minute rushes, we have a system in place where the Support Lead reviews and approves these requests on a case-by-case basis. This means that while you can't always count on getting a flexi holiday right away, the Support Lead will carefully consider each request to make sure everyone gets a fair turn. By having the Support Lead review these requests, we can balance everyone's needs and make sure this benefit is enjoyed by as many people as possible.

**TechMojo policy:** The Support Lead approves flexi holidays at their discretion. It's not always on a first-come, first-served basis to ensure fairness.

Our flexible holiday policy is designed to provide you with a good balance between work and personal time, while also ensuring that we can maintain our business operations without disruptions. Having a limit on the number of flexi holidays you can take in a month helps us achieve this balance, allowing you to recharge and come back to work refreshed, while also ensuring we have sufficient staff to meet our commitments. This limit also helps prevent over-absence in critical periods, which would impact our team's productivity and overall performance.

**TechMojo policy:** No, you are not eligible for more than 2 flexi holidays in a month.
Is there a deadline to apply for Flexi leave?

To ensure that we can make the necessary arrangements for your time off, we need to have a heads up about when you're planning to take a flexi holiday. That's why we require at least 1 week's notice, so we can plan and schedule accordingly. This helps us to make the most of our team's time and ensures that everyone's needs are met.

**TechMojo policy:** Yes, you must apply for a flexi holiday at least 1 week in advance.
Can I combine Flexi Holiday with my regular leave?

Flexi holidays are a special type of leave that allows you to take a day off on a day of your choice, as long as it's not a regular workday or a public holiday. This flexibility is what makes flexi holidays unique, and it's meant to give you more control over your time off. By not combining flexi holidays with regular leave, we can ensure that this special perk remains available for everyone to use as intended.

**TechMojo policy:** No, flexi holidays cannot be clubbed with regular leaves.

This policy helps us ensure fairness and consistency in how time off is used and managed across the company.

**TechMojo policy:** No, there is no carry forward or encashment available for Flexi holidays.

To ensure that our support team is always adequately staffed, we use a fair distribution approach to approve Flexi Holiday requests. This means that the support lead will review all requests and make decisions based on the overall team's availability, rather than simply approving requests on a first-come, first-served basis. This approach helps us maintain a balanced schedule and ensure that we have enough team members available to cover each shift.
